channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 2 men go free after their convictions were overturned in a case involving a discredited detective
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Kan. (AP) — Two men were freed Wednesday after their convictions were overturned in a 2009 double homicide whose investigation was overseen by a discredited white Kansas City, Kansas, police detective.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fire destroys historic Northern California mansion
item.description: The historic Bidwell Mansion in Chico, California, a 156-year-old Victorian home, was badly damaged in an early morning fire.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: UN General Assembly demands a ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: The U.N. General Assembly overwhelmingly approved resolutions Wednesday demanding an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and expressing support for the U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees that Israel has moved to ban.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rights group accuses Russian mercenaries of abuses against civilians in Mali
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Mali’s armed forces, supported by Russian mercenaries, committed abuses against civilians since the withdrawal of a U.N. peacekeeping mission late last year, Human Rights Watch said in a report released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Turning dusty attic treasures into cash can yield millions for some and disappointment for others
item.description: THOMASTON, Maine (AP) — Kaja Veilleux has been hunting New England attic treasures for more than 50 years.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woody Allen and Soon-Yi Previn are sued by the personal chef they fired
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Woody Allen 's former personal chef claims in a lawsuit that the filmmaker and his wife fired him because of his service in the U.S. Army Reserves and questions about his pay, then “rubbed salt on the wounds” by saying they didn't like his cooking.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Arizona AG sues Saudi firm over 'excessive' groundwater pumping, saying it's a public nuisance
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes announced Wednesday she's suing a Saudi Arabian agribusiness for allegedly violating a public nuisance law, contending that its groundwater pumping threatens the public health, safety and infrastructure of local communities in a rural western county
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dick Van Dyke, Cher among those forced to flee Malibu homes in wake of wildfire
item.description: Officials said the fire, which grew significantly overnight, was only 7% contained as of Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, NewsNation

item.title: Private Sin: How a Michigan church protected child molesters
item.description: Protestant Reformed Churches in America is struggling with what it has called the "private sin" of child sexual abuse and decades of covering up for abusers, including church leaders and school teachers.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, NewsNation

item.title: Luxury real estate brokers charged in federal indictment with sex trafficking in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two luxury real estate brokers and their brother have been charged with luring, drugging and violently raping dozens of women over more than a decade, according to a federal indictment unsealed Wednesday in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australian Shepherd makes 'miraculous' recovery after ingesting lethal dose of pills
item.description: “We started the treatment with a very poor prognosis,” Dr. Lindsey McGuire said. “We didn’t expect her to survive.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing could return to New York on a governor's warrant
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The suspect in the killing of the UnitedHealthcare CEO plans to fight extradition to New York to face murder charges, but officials hope to get him back with what’s called a governor’s warrant. The process could happen quickly or take more than a month. New York Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A search for the perfect match, North Carolina six-year-old in need of donor
item.description: On October 17, Da Silva said doctors with Duke diagnosed Louise with a rare disease called Severe Aplastic Anemia.
item.dc:creator: Mariah Ellis, NewsNation

item.title: Albertsons sues Kroger for failing to win approval of their proposed supermarket merger
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons' plan for the largest U.S. supermarket merger in history crumbled Wednesday, with Albertsons pulling out of the $24.6 billion deal and the two companies accusing each other of not doing enough to push their proposed alliance through.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: House approves mammoth annual defense policy bill
item.description: The House passed the annual defense policy bill Wednesday, sending the mammoth measure to the Senate for consideration ahead of Congress’s end-of-month deadline.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tennessee DA's office adds another law enforcement officer to Giglio List
item.description: The list is comprised of law enforcement officers who have been found to be untruthful or had other issues calling their credibility into question.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: US inflation ticked up last month as some price pressures remain persistent
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Fueled by pricier used cars, hotel rooms and groceries, inflation in the United States moved slightly higher last month in the latest sign that some price pressures remain elevated.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bernie Sanders says this will likely be his last term in Senate
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders has served in Congress since 1991, spending 16 years in the House before being elected to the Senate in 2006.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: United Healthcare CEO killing: Suspect used 'ghost gun,' police say
item.description: 'Ghost guns' have been a growing concern for years now, and Mangione's arrest Monday put them back in the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: The Amazing Kreskin, renowned mentalist and frequent late-night guest, dies at 89
item.description: The Amazing Kreskin, an entertainer and mentalist who rose to fame amid frequent appearances on TV talk shows like “The Tonight Show” and “The Mike Douglas Show,” has died, his family confirmed on social media. He was 89 years old.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Cornyn warns Hegseth faces 'unpleasant' confirmation hearing
item.description: Sen. John Cornyn (R-Texas) says Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s pick to head the Department of Defense, faces a nasty Senate confirmation hearing that would likely be uglier than the battle over Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Luigi Mangione's fingerprints match water bottle found near shooting: sources
item.description: Fingerprints taken from Luigi Mangione in Pennsylvania matched those on a water bottle found near the scene of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's fatal shooting, sources said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Malibu wildfire prompts evacuation orders and warnings for 20,000, including Dick Van Dyke and Cher
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — Weather conditions were forecast to improve in Southern California, aiding firefighters in their battle against a wind-driven wildfire that has forced up to 20,000 people, including Cher, Dick Van Dyke and his wife, from their homes in Malibu.
item.dc:creator: JAE C. HONG, ERIC THAYER and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Daniel Penny defense eyes Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg for potential malicious prosecution lawsuit
item.description: Daniel Penny's defense team is floating the possibility of a malicious prosecution lawsuit against Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg after Penny's acquittal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, team trail Fauci on trust as medical information source: Survey
item.description: Trump and other Republicans have criticized Fauci, who was the face of the first Trump and Biden administration’s response against COVID-19, over his handling of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court dismisses Nvidia’s appeal in securities lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday dismissed an appeal from Nvidia in a securities lawsuit against the chipmaking giant, allowing the case to move forward. The court dismissed the appeal as “improvidently granted” in an unsigned, one-sentence opinion without explanation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Albertsons gives up on Kroger merger, sues the grocery chain
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons in 2022 proposed what would be the largest grocery store merger in U.S. history. The companies said a merger would help them better compete with big retailers like Walmart, Costco and Amazon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The burial site of the people Andrew Jackson enslaved was lost. The Hermitage says it is found
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — At least 26 enslaved people died on the Tennessee plantation of President Andrew Jackson between 1804 and the end of the Civil War in 1865. Where they were laid to rest is knowledge that had been lost to time.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Inflation ticks higher as Fed eyes another rate cut
item.description: Inflation picked up slightly in November, according to data released Wednesday by the Labor Department, but likely not by enough to derail the Federal Reserve's plans for another rate cut.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kayaker who faked his death, fled the US charged after returning to Wisconsin
item.description: Police had previously said they believed Borgwardt planned his Aug. 12 disappearance and tried to conceal his efforts to mislead investigations.
item.dc:creator: Indiana Schilz, Fox 5

item.title: DA suggests unusual idea for halting Trump’s hush money case while upholding his conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Eager to preserve President-elect Donald Trump ’s hush money conviction even as he returns to office, prosecutors are suggesting various ways forward — including one based on how some courts handle criminal cases when defendants die. Trump’s spokesperson called the ideas “pathetic.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida state lawmaker files measure eliminating tuition aid for undocumented immigrants
item.description: Florida state Sen. Randy Fine (R) announced Monday he was introducing a bill to limit college tuition aid to undocumented immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: McConnell receives medical attention after falling
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) received medical attention after falling following a Republican lunch meeting Tuesday. A spokesman for McConnell said the GOP leader tripped after the lunch. "He sustained a minor cut to the face and sprained his wrist.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas judge dismisses infamous 'Black Widow' murder case after 27 years
item.description: The judge dismissed murder charges against Margaret Rudin, the woman dubbed "The Black Widow" who a jury convicted of killing her husband more than two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KRON4

item.title: McDonald's in Pennsylvania slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: A McDonald's in Altoona, Pennsylvania, is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KRON4

item.title: Video shows thieves speed off in sports cars from Michigan dealership
item.description: A group of suspected car thieves sped away from a Michigan dealership in rare sports vehicles, leading police on a high-speed chase that ended with the arrest of a 19-year-old Detroit woman.
item.dc:creator: Duncan MacLean, KRON4

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9
